overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a presence on Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research and opportunities in computational social science, disinformation studies, and related areas. His LinkedIn profile has the largest following, while Bluesky shows good repost engagement. Overall engagement is moderate and reflects a professional audience interested in academic research and job opportunities.

contentThemes.description: Focuses on Fabio Giglietto's research related to computational social science, including detecting coordinated link sharing and information operations.
Content related to disinformation across multiple platforms and methodological approaches to studying it.
Posts announcing research grants, job openings (postdoctoral researchers), and PhD student opportunities in computational social science.
Announcements about new papers and upcoming presentations at conferences.

recommendations: Increase posting frequency, especially on Mastodon, to improve visibility.
Tailor content to each platform's audience. LinkedIn for professional announcements, Bluesky for research updates and community engagement, and Mastodon for broader discussions.
Use relevant hashtags to improve discoverability and reach a wider audience.
Engage with comments and replies to foster a stronger community around the topics.
Incorporate visual elements, such as images or videos, to increase engagement.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky exhibits good engagement, particularly with reposts. Focus on sharing research publications and engaging in discussions related to disinformation.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon has relatively lower engagement. Consider posting more frequently and engaging with the Mastodon community. Focus on open-source projects and algorithm development.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the highest follower count and good engagement in terms of likes and comments. Continue to use this platform for professional announcements, hiring opportunities, and research grants.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
